title: Digitalis mauretanica
intro: Digitalis mauretanica, also known as Moroccan foxglove, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the plantain family (Plantaginaceae). The health benefits of Digitalis mauretanica include its ability to lower blood pressure, improve heart function, and reduce inflammation. The bioactive constituents of this plant include glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Digitalis mauretanica include tinctures, infusions, and capsules, which are used to treat cardiovascular and circulatory issues, as well as to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation.
taxonomy: Digitalis mauretanica is classified within the family Plantaginaceae. It belongs to the genus Digitalis, which is commonly known as foxglove. The genus Digitalis is a group of flowering plants native to the Mediterranean region. Digitalis mauretanica is a species of flowering plant that is native to Morocco. This species is closely related to other Digitalis species, which are known for their tubular flowers.

side_effects: Unfortunately, there is limited information available about Digitalis mauretanica. However, Digitalis mauretanica, like other plants in the Digitalis genus, may contain cardiac glycosides that can be toxic to humans and animals if ingested in large quantities. If ingested, these compounds can cause nausea, vomiting, and arrhythmias, among other symptoms. In severe cases, digitalis toxicity can lead to cardiac arrest and even death. More research is needed to fully understand the potential side effects of Digitalis mauretanica.
